content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: November 21, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Dwight

Subject: Real Estate Salesperson's Source of Income - Seller v. Buyer

The following question was posed by the city attorney of Blue Lake, Richard Platz, regarding the source of income of a real estate salesperson. To whom does the real estate salesperson attribute his/her income if:

1) Each individual, the seller and buyer, are both represented by their individual salesperson.

2) If one real estate salesperson represents both the seller and buyer when finalizing the sale of a piece of real estate.

The advice rendered to Mr. Platz was the following. With respect to a seller and buyer who are represented by their own real estate agent, the real estate salesperson's source of income shall be attributed to the source which he/she represented in the transaction. Therefore, the real estate person who represented the seller shall list the seller as his/her source of income; and the real estate salesperson representing the buyer shall attribute his/her income to the buyer.

With respect to the second transaction where the real estate agent represents both the seller and the buyer, the salesperson's source of income is both the seller and buyer on an equally proportionate basis. Therefore, if the standard agreement allows for a 6% commission arising out of the real estate sale to go to the salesperson handling the transaction, then 3% shall be attributed to the buyer and 3% to the seller.

DED:nrw
